master_row.title: Theorem 1: Wavelength Quantization: oint_S^1 K· Dl
master_row.canonical_statement: Closed-loop phase matching on $S^{1}$ requires \[ \oint_{S^{1}} \mathbf{k}\cdot d\mathbf{l} = 2\pi n \quad\Rightarrow\quad n\lambda = 2\pi R_\oplus. \] For $n = 2$ tidal bulges (gravitationally bound opposite poles), \[ \lambda = \pi R_\oplus \approx 2.0015\times 10^{4}\,\mathrm{km}. \]
master_row.formation_note: Recovered from a D:\All source document under ‘Theorem 1: Wavelength Quantization’.
master_row.dictionary: Closed-loop phase matching on S^1 requires For n = 2 tidal bulges (gravitationally bound opposite poles)
master_row.encyclopedia: Theorem 1: Wavelength Quantization: oint_S^1 K· Dl is a contour integral, written oint_S^1 k· dl = 2π n Rightarrow nλ = 2π R_oplus.. It is evaluated by integrating along a closed contour, capturing global or topological properties of the field. It is built from π, λ, n, R, defining oint_S^1 k· dl. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
